authoritative_sources.title: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. Its order-merchandise rule governs shipment promises, delay notices, cancellations, prompt refunds, and order-record expectations when sellers cannot meet advertised fulfillment commitments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: order acceptance, delay handling, cancellation packaging, and pickup or delivery commitment control
used_for: prompt-refund expectations when delivery commitments fail

authoritative_sources.title: A Businessperson's Guide to Federal Warranty Law
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's warranty business guide is the authoritative public federal baseline for warranty disclosure obligations, seller versus warrantor boundaries, and remedy communication limits under federal warranty law.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: warranty-sensitive order handling and remedy-boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1723
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California Legislature publishes the controlling statutory text on posted refund policies and fallback refund or equal-exchange obligations for covered retail sales in California.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. federal consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance. The page explains customer dispute timelines and when unresolved merchant refund or tender problems should move into payment-dispute handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch, refund-not-posted handling, and customer handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by many U.S. jurisdictions for retail price verification and price-accuracy controls. It is authoritative for handling conflicts between advertised, shelf, and scanned prices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price-evidence review and promotion conflict handling
used_for: shelf and POS price conflict discipline
public baseline for price accuracy controls

authoritative_sources.title: Recall Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. regulator for consumer product safety. Its retailer-facing checklist gives public workflow expectations for identifying recalled products, blocking sale, isolating inventory, using customer records, and coordinating refunds or credits during a recall.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: store-level stop-sale, customer-impact control, and release prevention
used_for: use of customer records for recall outreach

authoritative_sources.title: Reverse Logistics Brochure
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA is the primary U.S. federal authority for hazardous-material transportation. Its reverse-logistics guidance is directly relevant to retail operations and explicitly states that lithium cells and batteries are not eligible for the reverse-logistics exception.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: damaged-device, battery, and transport stop-condition handling

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcode usage. Its General Specifications are authoritative for UPC, GTIN, and related item-identity handling across POS, OMS, inventory, and pickup workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SKU, UPC, model, serial, and barcode record-integrity review

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm store, channel, customer, order or reservation identifier, product family, and tenant authority context before accepting the work and determine whether the request is in scope, out of scope, or blocked.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Order, payment, and identity prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify order state, tender status, financing posture, customer verification, SKU and model linkage, and serial or IMEI capture so retail execution does not proceed on stale or mismatched inputs.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Reservation, pickup, and service-dependency execution
workflow_stages.description: Review inventory reservation, cage or will-call release conditions, pickup windows, delivery or install dependencies, and customer-notification status to determine whether the work can safely advance.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Price, refund, and payment exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Move price conflicts, refund or cancellation exceptions, billing mismatches, and financed-order edge cases into explicit blocked or escalated states with owner routing rather than silent closure.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, stop-sale, and battery-safety boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve loss-prevention holds, recall boundaries, stop-sale instructions, and damaged-battery handling whenever customer release or reverse logistics becomes unsafe or suspicious.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when release scans, customer acknowledgment, inventory reservation updates, and tenant-specific exception rules have been satisfied and recorded.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: order confirmations, reservations, pickup queues, will-call records, delivery-slot references, and customer account records
POS receipts, payment ledgers, split-tender records, financing references, promotion records, refund-policy notes, and service-recovery records
SKU, UPC, GTIN, model-number, serial-number, IMEI, attach-item, open-box, and inventory-reservation records
service tickets, setup or install dependencies, release scans, customer acknowledgments, and completion notes
recall notices, stop-sale directives, quarantine logs, damaged-device notes, and hazmat handling instructions
fraud or loss-prevention holds, tender-exception notes, chargeback-adjacent case references, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: service desk or install scheduling system

allowed: Classify electronics-retail requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, customer-verification, serial, service, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, fraud adjudication, safety disposition, or legal interpretation.
A tenant, lender, carrier, manufacturer, or jurisdictional policy materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, fraud, recall, financing, or policy exception changes whether execution may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of release, completion, or reservation disposition.
Override pricing, refund, or safety controls without documented authority.
Release product to pickup, delivery, or sale when payment, recall, or fraud evidence remains unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: OMS, POS, and pickup records conflict and the lane releases a high-value item anyway
a refund, cancellation, or goodwill concession is promised without delegated pricing or guest-services authority
a recalled or damaged battery device is handed to a customer or shipped through routine reverse logistics
serial, IMEI, or customer-verification evidence is missing and the lane still closes the release as complete
loss-prevention indicators are treated as ordinary service friction and the lane bypasses the hold
high-volume holiday queues collapse distinct exception classes into generic completion
tenant-specific financing, franchise, or jurisdiction rules are ignored in favor of a generic retail answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State refund-policy rules, private-label financing terms, franchise carve-outs, and manufacturer or carrier release rules can materially differ across jurisdictions and products.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses FTC, CFPB, California legislative, NIST, CPSC, PHMSA, and GS1 sources as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, lender, franchise, customer type, and product-category facts before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, pricing and refund boundaries, payment handling, item identity, and stop-condition behavior instead of only consumer-protection policy.

The lane stops at retail execution. Final pricing exceptions, goodwill concessions, fraud findings, warranty adjudication, and safety or hazmat signoff stay outside the lane even when customer pressure is high.
